title: Searching a Reasoning Space
concept: Representing reasoning steps as branches in a tree, letting the agent explore multiple paths concurrently.

exercises.prompt: New unit! Quick guess: what does a "tree" usually mean in computing?

options.text: A structure that branches out into many paths from one starting point
A single straight line with no choices

exercises.explain: In computing, a tree starts from one point and splits into branches, each leading to more branches. That branching shape is exactly what lets an AI explore many possible ideas, which is the heart of Tree of Thoughts.

cards.title: Not just one guess
cards.body: Imagine solving a puzzle where you try one idea, hit a wall, and start over. What if you could explore several ideas at once instead? That is what Tree of Thoughts lets an AI do.

cards.title: What is Tree of Thoughts?
cards.body: Tree of Thoughts (ToT) is a way for an AI to reason by branching. Each reasoning step becomes a branch, and the AI explores multiple paths at the same time instead of committing to one.

cards.title: Like a road trip with maps
cards.body: Instead of driving down one road and hoping it works, you look at several roads on a map first. You try promising ones and back out of dead ends. ToT explores reasoning the same way.

cards.title: A number puzzle
cards.body: Say the AI must reach 24 using some numbers. It branches: 'try adding first', 'try multiplying first', 'try subtracting first'. Each branch grows more steps until one path succeeds.

cards.title: Myth: more branches = smarter
cards.body: Exploring many paths helps, but too many wastes time and effort. Good ToT trims weak branches early so the AI spends energy only on promising ideas.

cards.title: One path vs many paths
cards.body: A normal chain of thought follows a single line of steps. A tree of thoughts branches into several lines and compares them.

left.text: One line of reasoning, step after step, no going back.

right.text: Many branches explored at once, keeping the best paths.

exercises.prompt: In Tree of Thoughts, what does each branch represent?

options.text: A possible reasoning step or path the AI could take
A final answer with no other options
A word the AI is not allowed to use

exercises.explain: Each branch is a possible reasoning step. By growing many branches, the AI can explore different paths and pick the one that leads to a good answer.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Tree of Thoughts lets an AI explore several reasoning paths instead of just one.
exercises.explain: True. The whole point of ToT is branching, so the AI can try multiple paths at once and compare them rather than being stuck on a single line of thinking.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank about how ToT works.
exercises.sentence: In Tree of Thoughts, each reasoning step becomes a branch the AI can explore.
exercises.explain: Each reasoning step becomes a branch. Growing and exploring these branches is what lets the AI search a whole space of possible ideas.

exercises.prompt: Which explanation of Tree of Thoughts sounds AI-generated (smooth and complete)?

options.text: Tree of Thoughts models reasoning as a branching tree, letting an agent explore several candidate paths concurrently and keep the most promising ones.
uh its like when the ai just picks one thing and goes idk
tree thing = many branch. good i think??

exercises.explain: The first option is polished, precise, and complete, which is typical of AI writing. The others are casual and unsure, like a quick human note.

exercises.prompt: An AI is solving a tricky riddle. Using Tree of Thoughts, what is the BEST description of how it works?

options.text: It branches into several reasoning paths, explores them, trims weak ones, and keeps the most promising path to an answer.
It picks the very first idea and never reconsiders it.
It grows every possible branch forever without ever choosing.
It refuses to reason and just guesses randomly.

exercises.explain: Tree of Thoughts turns reasoning into a branching search: the AI explores multiple paths at once, drops dead ends, and follows the strongest branch. That balance of exploring and trimming is what makes it powerful, unlike sticking to one guess or exploring endlessly.
